exercises.title: Trace HashMap basics
Frequency counting pattern
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blank to complete the frequency counter.

blanks.hint: Returns value or a fallback if key absent

hints: getOrDefault(key, defaultValue) — returns value if key exists, default otherwise

exercises.title: Simple HashMap operations
exercises.scenario: Build and query a student score map.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Map<String, Integer>` with student scores. Then:
- Print Priya's score
- Update Raj's score to 92
- Check if Vikram is in the map
- Print final size

Expected output:
Priya: 88
Vikram present: false
Size: 3
hints: size() returns number of unique keys

exercises.title: Word frequency counter
exercises.instructions:
Count word frequencies in a sentence. Print all words and counts (sort keys for deterministic output).

Input: 'the cat sat on the mat the cat'

Expected output:
cat: 2
mat: 1
on: 1
sat: 1
the: 3
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Word frequency counter with sorted output
hints: Split by space to get words array

exercises.title: HashMap as lookup table
exercises.instructions:
Build a map of country capitals. Then answer 3 queries.

Expected output:
India: New Delhi
Japan: Tokyo
Xyzland: Unknown

test_cases.expected_stdout:
India: New Delhi
Japan: Tokyo
Xyzland: Unknown

exercises.title: Iterate entrySet — print key-value pairs
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Map<String, Double>` of exchange rates (USD, EUR, JPY against INR). Iterate using entrySet() and print each.

Expected output (sorted for determinism):
EUR = 89.5
JPY = 0.55
USD = 83.2
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Exchange rate map with entrySet iteration
hints: keySet() gives all keys as a Set

exercises.title: Character frequency in string
exercises.scenario: Count character occurrences with Map<Character, Integer>.
exercises.instructions:
Count character frequencies in a string (case-insensitive, letters only). Find and print the most frequent character.

Input: 'Hello World'

Expected output:
Most frequent: l (3 times)
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Character frequency — most frequent char
hints: Iterate entrySet() to find max value

exercises.title: Group students by grade
exercises.instructions:
Group student names by their grade letter. Print each group sorted.

Students: Aisha(A), Raj(B), Priya(A), Vikram(B), Anita(C), Dev(A)

Expected output:
A: [Aisha, Dev, Priya]
B: [Raj, Vikram]
C: [Anita]
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Group students by grade using computeIfAbsent

test_cases.expected_stdout:
A: [Aisha, Dev, Priya]
B: [Raj, Vikram]
C: [Anita]

hints: Sort both the grade keys and each student list for output

exercises.title: Object lookup by ID
exercises.scenario: HashMap as object cache for O(1) retrieval.
exercises.instructions:
Create `Employee` with id (int) and name. Build a `Map<Integer, Employee>` catalog. Then look up employee 102 (exists) and 999 (doesn't exist).

Expected output:
Found: Raj (id=102)
Not found: null
hints: Map<Integer, Employee> — key is the ID
get(999) returns null — not in map

exercises.title: Two-sum problem
exercises.scenario: Classic algorithm problem — O(n) solution with HashMap.
exercises.instructions:
Given an array of integers and a target sum, find two indices where nums[i] + nums[j] == target.

Use a HashMap to solve in O(n): for each element, check if (target - element) is already in the map.

nums = [2, 7, 11, 15], target = 9

Expected output:
Indices: [0, 1]
hints: For each element, complement = target - element
If complement is in the map, we found our pair
seen maps value → index for previous elements
O(n): one pass, one map lookup per element

exercises.title: Sales aggregation by product
exercises.instructions:
Given arrays of product names and quantities sold, aggregate total sales by product.

products: [Mouse, Keyboard, Mouse, Monitor, Keyboard, Mouse]
qty:       [2,     1,        3,     1,       2,        1]

Print total per product (sorted), then the highest-selling product.

Expected output:
Keyboard: 3
Monitor: 1
Mouse: 6
Best seller: Mouse (6)
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Sales aggregation — sum quantities by product

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Keyboard: 3
Monitor: 1
Mouse: 6
Best seller: Mouse (6)

hints: Iterate entrySet() to find max value

exercises.title: Trace containsKey and getOrDefault
Anagram detector
exercises.scenario: HashMap-based anagram check — classic interview problem.
exercises.instructions:
Two strings are anagrams if they use the same characters with the same frequencies.

Write `isAnagram(String a, String b)` using character frequency maps.

Test with: (listen, silent)=true, (hello, world)=false, (Aisha, Aisha)=true

Expected output:
listen/silent: true
hello/world: false
Aisha/Aisha: true

test_cases.expected_stdout:
listen/silent: true
hello/world: false
Aisha/Aisha: true

hints: Build frequency map for each string
Two strings are anagrams if their frequency maps are EQUAL
Map.equals() compares contents — works perfectly here

exercises.title: Order management system with HashMap
exercises.scenario: Real domain — orders looked up by ID, grouped by status.
exercises.instructions:
Create `Order` (orderId int, customerName String, total double, status String).

Build `OrderManager`:
- `Map<Integer, Order>` for O(1) lookup by orderId
- `addOrder(Order)`, `findById(int)`, `getByStatus(String)` returning List<Order>
- `totalRevenue()` — sum of DELIVERED order totals

Create 5 orders (mix of PENDING/DELIVERED), demonstrate all operations.

Expected output:
Order #102: Raj - ₹1999.0 [DELIVERED]
DELIVERED orders: 2
Revenue: ₹4799.0
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// Order and OrderManager using HashMap
hints: getByStatus: loop values(), filter by status
totalRevenue: loop values(), sum where status==DELIVERED

exercises.title: Inventory tracker with three HashMap patterns
exercises.scenario: All three patterns in one real system.
exercises.instructions:
Build an `InventoryTracker` for a warehouse:

1. `Map<String, Integer>` skuToStock — stock levels by SKU
2. `Map<String, String>` skuToCategory — category by SKU  
3. `recordSale(String sku, int qty)` — reduces stock (throw if insufficient)
4. `restock(String sku, int qty)` — increases stock
5. `lowStock(int threshold)` → List<String> of SKUs with stock <= threshold
6. `totalByCategory()` → Map<String, Integer> total stock per category

Test with 5 SKUs across 2 categories, sell some, find low stock.

Expected output:
Low stock (<= 10): [SKU-002, SKU-004]
Electronics total: 85
Books total: 45
exercises.starter_code:
import java.util.*;

// InventoryTracker with 3 HashMap patterns

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Low stock (<= 10): [SKU-002, SKU-004]
Books total: 35
Electronics total: 80

hints: Two maps: one for stock, one for category
recordSale: getOrDefault for current stock, validate, put(sku, new amount)
lowStock: iterate entrySet, filter by value <= threshold
totalByCategory: use skuToCategory to find category, accumulate in new map
